Flagship Product

Amoris AIOS

Not one agent. An operating system. Multiple agents, shared memory, one command centre that gives you visibility and control over everything they're doing.

Individual agents are entry points. AIOS is where clients graduate when they want the agents to work together, not in silos. "Start with one agent. Grow into an operating system."

Book a Discovery Call → See Pricing
The AIOS Difference

Agents working together, not in silos.

Shared Memory

Every agent knows what the others know.

Your CRM Copilot knows who your AI Receptionist booked. Your daily brief knows what your Reporting Agent flagged. Context flows across the system.

One Dashboard

Single view of your entire AI operation.

Calls handled, chats resolved, leads qualified, reports sent — all in one place. You see the system working, not just individual outputs.

Vault

Your business knowledge, centralised.

All agents draw from the same approved vault — your SOPs, pricing, policies, playbooks. Update once, every agent knows immediately.

Packages

Two ways to start.

AIOS Core

One department, 3–5 agents.

Best for: one team, one workflow, one problem to solve well. Sales, operations, or customer support — pick the function, we build the system.

  • ✓ 3–5 custom agents
  • ✓ Shared memory vault
  • ✓ Workflow automations
  • ✓ 1 integration stack
  • ✓ Monthly reporting
  • ✓ Slack-based command centre
Investment
₹3.5L–6L setup + ₹40k/mo $7k–12k setup + $800/mo
Start with AIOS Core →
AIOS Company

Multi-department, custom per team.

Best for: companies that want AI running across sales, ops, and customer experience — with each team having their own agents and a shared central vault.

  • ✓ Unlimited agents across departments
  • ✓ Cross-department shared memory
  • ✓ Custom workflows per team
  • ✓ Full integration stack
  • ✓ Team training & documentation
  • ✓ Dedicated expansion roadmap
Investment
₹8L–15L setup + ₹75k–1.25L/mo $15k–30k setup + $1.5k–2.5k/mo
Explore AIOS Company →
FAQ

Questions about AIOS.

What's the difference between individual agents and AIOS?
An individual agent does one specific job — your AI Receptionist answers calls, your WhatsApp Agent handles messages. AIOS is the layer that connects them: shared memory so each agent knows what the others have done, one dashboard to see it all, and a command centre to manage and expand the system.
How long does AIOS take to deploy?
AIOS Core (one department, 3–5 agents): 6–8 weeks. AIOS Company (multi-department): 10–14 weeks. Timeline depends on integration complexity and how ready your data and documentation is.
Do I need to start with AIOS or can I start with one agent?
Start with one agent. Most clients deploy 1–2 agents first, see results, then graduate to AIOS when they want the system-level view and cross-agent memory. Setup fees for agents are credited toward AIOS.
What does 'shared memory' mean?
If your AI Receptionist books an appointment for a customer, your CRM Copilot already knows about it before the sales call starts. If your Reporting Agent noticed a dip in a metric last Tuesday, your daily brief mentions it. Agents share context, not just data.
What's the difference between AIOS Core and AIOS Company?
AIOS Core is one department: 3–5 agents, one team's workflows, one vault. Great starting point. AIOS Company is multi-department: custom agents per team, cross-department workflows, and training sessions for your team to manage and expand the system themselves.

Not sure which to start with?

Most clients start with one agent. We'll tell you on the call whether AIOS Core makes sense for your situation.
